name: madhubani-animal-tee-poses
description: The four canonical poses used across every animal in the Madhubani tee catalog. Each animal × pose pair produces one design. The set of four for any one animal is the standard 'mini-collection' unit.
v2_changes_2026_05_20: Expanded body_type_overrides to cover all 12 body types in animals.json (was: 4). Strengthened bird overrides (peacock previously rendered standing instead of perched; the new wording uses 'roosting on a low branch' and explicitly says 'NOT standing on ground'). Added overrides for crocodilian, small-mammal, stocky-omnivore, arboreal-primate, armored-quadruped, and tightened the existing bird block. The 4 slot names (standing-alert/seated-rest/signature-action/frontal-portrait) are CATALOG POSITIONS, not literal pose descriptions — the body-type override is the source of truth for what the rendered pose actually IS for that species.
rationale: Four catalog slots × per-body-type semantics. Slot names are catalog positions (every animal renders 1-4); the body_type_overrides map defines what each slot MEANS for each body type. A 'seated-rest' bird is PERCHED on a branch (not seated on its haunches); a 'seated-rest' serpent is COILED with hood lowered; a 'seated-rest' cetacean is SUSPENDED motionless; a 'seated-rest' mammal is actually seated. The build_subject_string function in bin/forge_madhubani.py consults body_type_overrides FIRST; the per-slot subject_template is the fallback only when no body-type override applies. 'Walking' is deliberately NOT a slot because v2 testing showed walking quadrupeds in side profile drop the far legs more often than they keep them.

poses.summary: Full-body side profile, all legs/limbs planted, head looking forward or slightly toward camera, calm dignified expression. The catalog's flagship pose — every animal must be recognizable from this image alone.
anatomy_emphasis: both legs visible on visible perch (birds)
poses.subject_template: single centered {animal_subject} STANDING ALERT in complete full-body side profile facing right, [body_type_anatomy_clause], [eye_character_clause: watchful ceremonial gravity], mouth closed, [body_fill_color_clause], decorated INSIDE with seven distinct zones of hand-drawn multi-color Madhubani ornament, body BETWEEN zones remains clean color field, bold hand-drawn double-contour keylines with weight variation, dotted Mithila ground line below the feet, 8-12 small ornamental flourishes in negative space

poses.summary: Resting position appropriate to the species: cats and primates seated with forelimbs upright; serpents coiled with hood lowered; birds perched with feet folded; large herbivores reclining (camel-fold for hooved animals). Quieter, more contemplative.
anatomy_emphasis: tail visible in natural rest curl
poses.subject_template: single centered {animal_subject} [rest_pose_for_species] in side profile facing right, [body_type_anatomy_clause], [eye_character_clause: contemplative stillness], mouth closed, [body_fill_color_clause], decorated INSIDE with seven distinct zones of hand-drawn Madhubani ornament, body BETWEEN zones remains clean color field, bold hand-drawn double-contour keylines with weight variation, dotted Mithila ground mark of paired peepal leaves beneath the body

poses.summary: The single most iconic action for that species. Tiger crouched ready to leap. Peacock fanned tail. Cobra reared with hood spread. Elephant trumpeting trunk raised. Eagle wings spread. Hummingbird hovering. Rhino charging.
anatomy_emphasis: characteristic action read instantly from silhouette
poses.subject_template: single centered {animal_subject} [signature_action_for_species] in dynamic side profile facing right, [body_type_anatomy_clause], [eye_character_clause: focused {action_intensity}], [body_fill_color_clause], decorated INSIDE with seven zones of hand-drawn Madhubani ornament emphasizing forward motion, body BETWEEN zones remains clean color field, bold hand-drawn double-contour keylines with weight variation, dotted Mithila ground mark appropriate to action (dust dots for charging, water dots for diving, etc.)

poses.summary: Head-and-shoulders frontal mark facing the viewer directly. Both almond eyes visible, decorative crown/halo motif above. Rewards close inspection. Best for back-of-tee placement or as a pocket companion to a full-body pose.
anatomy_emphasis: shoulders visible at bottom of frame
symmetric ornament left-right with handmade folk irregularity
poses.subject_template: single centered {animal_subject} FRONTAL PORTRAIT head-and-shoulders facing the viewer directly (full frontal symmetric composition), [signature_features_clause], both almond eyes carrying ANCIENT RITUAL PRESENCE (sacred ceremonial gaze, temple-icon gravity), mouth closed in dignified composure, [body_fill_color_clause], decorated INSIDE with symmetric tikka/medallion/leaf-vein/dot-band ornaments, decoration is SYMMETRIC left-right with handmade folk-art irregularity (not mechanical mirror), bold hand-drawn double-contour keylines with weight variation, decorative aura halo of dots and small petals radiating outward from the head, no ground line (frontal portrait floats)

body_type_overrides._purpose: Per-body-type pose customizations. Mammalian default poses don't work for serpents, birds, cetaceans, etc. When forge_madhubani.py builds the subject string, it checks this map first — if a body-type-specific override exists for the (body_type, pose_slug) pair, that override REPLACES the generic action clause. Otherwise the generic clause from poses[].subject_template applies.

cetacean.standing-alert: GLIDING ALERT in horizontal side profile facing right, body extended straight, fins outstretched, head looking forward
cetacean.seated-rest: SUSPENDED MOTIONLESS in horizontal posture, body fully extended straight, fins relaxed, eye calm and contemplative
cetacean.signature-action: in characteristic swimming action — body in a wide graceful curve, gliding forward, fins extended
cetacean.frontal-portrait: HEAD-ON FRONTAL VIEW from directly in front of the animal, broad flat head facing the viewer, mouth visible at center, both eyes visible flanking the head, pectoral fins visible at the sides — NOT a side profile, NOT a 3/4 view, fully frontal and symmetric

serpent.standing-alert: REARED ALERT with hood spread frontal-facing, body coiled below, head high
serpent.seated-rest: COILED at rest with hood lowered, head resting on the coils, body in concentric circles
serpent.signature-action: STRIKING POSE with hood fully spread, body in S-curve
serpent.frontal-portrait: HEAD-ON FRONTAL VIEW of the hood spread wide, both eyes visible, spectacle pattern centered on the hood, mouth closed dignified

bird.standing-alert: STANDING ALERT in side profile on a perch or ground line, both legs visible and planted, head looking forward, tail held downward in resting position (NOT fanned, NOT in flight, NOT mid-display) — this is the STANDING hero pose, the bird is upright and watchful
bird.seated-rest: PERCHED LOW AND RESTING on a horizontal branch — body sinks low on the branch with feet GRIPPING from beneath, legs nearly hidden under the body, head turned slightly to one side, tail trailing softly behind, wings folded close against the body — explicitly NOT standing tall, NOT on the ground, the bird is RESTING on a tree branch with its body lowered to the branch line
bird.signature-action: in the species' characteristic SHOWY action — fanned-tail courtship display for peacock, spread-wing in-flight glide for crane/stork/skimmer, calling with throat-pouch extended for hornbill, etc. (per species' signature_action field) — the wow-shot of the bird in motion or display
bird.frontal-portrait: HEAD-AND-CHEST FRONTAL VIEW facing the viewer directly, head centered with crest visible above, both eyes visible flanking the bill, bill centered pointing forward, both wings folded against body visible at sides as flat folk panels with feather patterns — fully frontal symmetric

primate.standing-alert: STANDING ALERT in side profile, both legs planted and arms hanging or resting near the body, all four limbs visible and proportional, head looking forward with dignified gravity
primate.seated-rest: SEATED CALMLY in side profile on a ground or low perch, hands folded in lap or resting on knees, tail curved at rest around the body, head still alert (resting, not sleeping)
primate.signature-action: in characteristic action (leaping between branches for macaque / langur, climbing for hoolock-gibbon, etc.), all four limbs visible and proportional, dynamic but anatomically clear
primate.frontal-portrait: HEAD-AND-SHOULDERS FRONTAL VIEW facing the viewer directly, both eyes visible (almond eyes NOT round cartoon eyes), characteristic mane/face features symmetric, hands visible at chest if applicable

arboreal-primate.standing-alert: HANGING BY BOTH LONG ARMS from a horizontal branch in side profile, body extended downward, legs tucked or hanging straight, head turned to look forward — the gibbon-typical suspended pose
arboreal-primate.seated-rest: SEATED ON A BRANCH with one or both arms holding adjacent branches, body upright, head turned slightly, tail (where present) curled — resting between brachiation swings
arboreal-primate.signature-action: MID-SWING between branches with one long arm extended forward to grip the next branch and the other trailing back, body horizontal mid-air, legs tucked under the body — explosive brachiation motion
arboreal-primate.frontal-portrait: HEAD-AND-SHOULDERS FRONTAL VIEW with long arms framing the chest, both eyes visible (gibbon almond-eye with white brow-band), face symmetric, dignified expression

crocodilian.standing-alert: BASKING ALERT on a mud bank in side profile with body fully extended, all four short legs planted, head raised slightly, tail extended straight back, scales rendered as flat folk panels along back — the watchful resting predator
crocodilian.seated-rest: FLOATING HALF-SUBMERGED in still water in side profile, only the eyes and snout above the waterline (rendered as flat folk dots and lines for the water), the bulk of the body suggested as a long horizontal shape beneath — calm and motionless
crocodilian.signature-action: JAWS OPEN in defensive display, body planted on bank with tail curled to one side for balance, all teeth visible as folk-style triangular shapes, eyes alert — the warning pose
crocodilian.frontal-portrait: HEAD-ON FRONTAL VIEW of the broad snout pointed at the viewer, both eyes visible on top of the head, jaws closed in dignified composure, scales radiating symmetrically along the snout — a fierce but stilled icon

small-mammal.standing-alert: STANDING ALERT in side profile on all four legs (or upright on hind legs for mongoose/squirrel/red-panda), small body compact, head looking forward, tail held in characteristic position (bushy for fox/squirrel, ringed for red-panda)
small-mammal.seated-rest: CURLED INTO A RESTING BALL with tail wrapped around the body (squirrel / red-panda / fox), or LOW ON HAUNCHES at ease, head still alert but body relaxed — small-mammal contemplation
small-mammal.signature-action: in characteristic action — squirrel mid-leap between branches, fox pouncing forward on prey, mongoose rearing back from snake-confrontation, red-panda balancing on branch with tail extended — species-specific signature motion
small-mammal.frontal-portrait: HEAD-AND-SHOULDERS FRONTAL VIEW facing the viewer directly, both eyes visible (small almond eyes), ears prominent (large for fox, rounded for red-panda), characteristic ruff/markings symmetric

stocky-omnivore.standing-alert: STANDING ALERT in side profile on all four planted legs, broad shoulders and hips proportional, head lowered slightly with snout forward (boar) or raised (bear), short tail visible — the powerful low-slung silhouette
stocky-omnivore.seated-rest: SEATED on haunches with forelegs upright (sloth-bear) or LYING DOWN with body curled (wild-boar at rest), head still alert — the muscular animal at ease
stocky-omnivore.signature-action: in characteristic action — sloth-bear STANDING ON HIND LEGS reaching upward into a tree for honey (the iconic gesture), wild-boar CHARGING with head lowered and tusks forward, pygmy-hog LEAPING through tall grass — species' signature wow-shot
stocky-omnivore.frontal-portrait: HEAD-AND-SHOULDERS FRONTAL VIEW facing the viewer directly, broad face centered, both small eyes visible flanking the snout, large ears or chest blaze prominent (sloth-bear's V-shaped chest blaze especially), short snout extended forward

armored-quadruped.standing-alert: STANDING ALERT in side profile on all four planted legs, body horizontal with armored scales/plates running along the back rendered as flat folk panels, head extended forward, long tail trailing behind — pangolin or aardvark in alert posture
armored-quadruped.seated-rest: CURLED INTO A SCALED BALL (pangolin defensive coil) with the head and underbelly tucked inside, scales overlapping in a spiral folk pattern across the visible outer surface, or in lower-profile resting curl — defensive at rest
armored-quadruped.signature-action: WALKING with armor plates rippling along the back in motion, one front and one back leg extended forward, head down at ground sniffing for ants, long tail trailing — characteristic forage walk
armored-quadruped.frontal-portrait: HEAD-ON FRONTAL VIEW with the long pointed snout extended toward the viewer, scales radiating outward from the head along the back, small eyes flanking the snout, ears slightly back — fierce but stilled icon

seed_allocation.rationale: Per-set seed range gives 4 seeds per animal (one per pose), with room for one retry seed per pose. 100 seeds per animal supports 25 animals per 1000-seed block. Indian wildlife block: 8200-8999. African wildlife block: 9000-9799. Bird block: 9800-10599. Mythological/stretch block: 10600-10999.
